100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 6, 1926, a significant political shift occurred as the British Parliament enacted a new law to address the ongoing coal strike, aiming to stabilize the nation’s economy amidst mounting unrest. In Germany, the government faced increasing pressure as the hyperinflation crisis deepened, prompting calls for urgent reforms. Meanwhile, in Italy, Mussolini's regime continued to tighten its grip on power, suppressing dissent and promoting nationalist policies.
